what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 design artboard showing three mobile app state mockups for a ledger interface: empty, loading, and success, with shared typography, tokenized colors, crafted microcopy, and small decorative system marks.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono, then relies on /surface/styles/vars.css for tokenized spacing, type, color, radius, and surface values.
Centers a fixed-size .view on the page with a radial warm/cool background, faint blueprint grid accent, bottom hatch fade, and circular V mark.
Displays three 300x600 phone mockups in a horizontal row, each with a status bar, ledger header, centered state content, action label, and external role caption.
The empty state injects a ledger icon at runtime with DNA.org.icon('ledger',46).
The loading state uses an inline SVG with an animated dashed line between a circle and a gold rectangle.
The success state changes the phone background and uses a gold check ring with completion-oriented copy.
Defines an .errmark error visual even though no error state is rendered in the markup.
standouts: State language is expressed through visual treatment, role labels, and microcopy rather than generic app-state placeholders.
The loading state has actual SVG motion while the other states remain still.
The file frames app states as register-specific voice examples, not only UI mechanics.
conventions: Uses file-local CSS with shared design tokens from vars.css.
Uses absolute positioning and fixed artboard dimensions for presentation rather than responsive application layout.
Uses compact class names and tokenized CSS variables throughout.
Uses div-based visual controls for action labels rather than semantic buttons.
Combines static HTML, inline SVG animation, and a small runtime icon injection.
concerns: The composition is fixed at 1600x900 and may not adapt to smaller or fluid viewports.
The empty-state icon depends on /surface/runtime/organisms.js and DNA.org being available.
The action elements are styled divs, so they have no native button semantics if treated as interactive UI.
The .errmark class is defined but unused in this file.
notes: A presentation artboard for ledger app states where empty, loading, and success screens are voiced as distinct visual registers.
questions: Is the unused .errmark intended for a fourth error-state phone in this piece?
Are the action labels meant to be interactive controls or only static design labels?
Should this artboard remain fixed-size, or is a responsive version expected elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title and headings describe app states speaking in registers, and the file directly implements that as three ledger state phone mockups with distinct captions, copy, and treatments.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to demonstrate how a ledger product can express empty, loading, and success states through a consistent house voice, tokenized visual system, and small symbolic gestures.
novelty: The app-state examples are presented as expressive registers with craft-oriented ledger copy and role captions, rather than as neutral UI placeholders.
contribution: Contributes a compact visual grammar for ledger state screens: three phone-state patterns, register labels, motion treatment for progress, completion treatment for success, and token-based styling that can be retained as state-language material in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone HTML design artboard showing three ledger mobile app states, empty/loading/success, with tokenized styling, crafted copy, phone mockups, animated loading SVG, and runtime ledger icon injection.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for demonstrating how app states can carry a consistent ledger house voice through visual register, typography, color, motion, and microcopy.
